concerns.text: Task T010 and T011 are marked [P] (parallel-safe) but explicitly state '(Run after T012)'. T012 (Implementation) must complete before T010/T011 (Tests) can execute. Marking them as [P] implies they can run concurrently with T012 or each other without dependency, which is false. This violates the 'Producer before consumer' rule where the implementation artifact is the producer for the test consumer.

Task T018 is marked [P] but depends on T012 (Conflict Detector) and T020 (Conflict Agent). The 'Dependencies & Execution Order' section correctly notes T018 must wait for T012, but the [P] tag on T018 suggests it is parallel-safe with other Phase 4 tasks, ignoring the hard dependency on the US1 implementation. This creates a semantic contradiction in the parallelization strategy.

Task T030 (Validate full pipeline) is marked [P] but logically requires the completion of T024 (Run full experiment) and T029 (Generate report). A validation task cannot run in parallel with the generation of the artifacts it validates. The [P] tag is incorrect here as it implies no ordering dependency on the preceding analysis tasks.

Task T014 description mentions 'model sizes' in the API signature but the task list does not include a specific task to implement the logic for varying model sizes (only thresholds are listed in the description). While T014 covers the interface, the dependency on 'model sizes' for FR-008 is not explicitly broken down into a producer task if the model selection logic is separate from the threshold logic.
